title: The Alignment Matrix
concept: The alignment matrix plots the attention weights between input and output words, visualizing which words were aligned during translation.

exercises.prompt: The alignment matrix is built using values from which neural network component?
exercises.explain: Attention weights determine how much focus a model puts on different parts of an input sequence, which is what the alignment matrix visualizes.

cards.title: Connecting the Dots
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how an AI translator decides which words match up when languages use different sentence structures? It uses a visual cheat sheet called the alignment matrix to keep track of word connections!

cards.title: What is an Alignment Matrix?
cards.body: An alignment matrix is a grid that visualizes how much attention a model paid to each input word when generating each output word. Darker or brighter squares in the grid represent stronger connections (higher attention weights) between those words.

cards.title: The Spotlight Log
cards.body: Imagine a spotlight operator at a theater show. As the actors speak in French, the spotlight shines on the corresponding English actor on the stage. The alignment matrix is the operator's logbook, showing exactly which actor was in the spotlight at every moment of the play.

cards.title: Seeing Word-Order Swaps
cards.body: When translating the French phrase 'Le chat noir' to the English 'The black cat', the model needs to swap the order of the adjective and noun. The alignment matrix will show a bright spot connecting 'noir' with 'black', and 'chat' with 'cat', visualizing this word-order swap as a diagonal cross in the grid.

cards.title: Myth: Translation is Linear
cards.body: Many people think translation models translate words strictly one-by-one in a straight line. In reality, languages have different word orders, and the alignment matrix proves that the model looks ahead and backward to connect the right words together.

cards.title: Visualizing Alignment Patterns
cards.body: See how the alignment matrix changes when word order is identical versus when it is reversed.

left.text: Bright squares form a straight diagonal line because the word order matches exactly.

right.text: Bright squares form a crossed pattern, showing the model swapped the word positions.

exercises.prompt: What does the brightness of a square in an alignment matrix tell us?

options.text: The spelling difficulty of the words
The strength of the attention weight between two words
The speed at which the words were translated
The number of syllables in the words

exercises.explain: The brightness or darkness of a cell in the alignment matrix shows the attention strength (weight) between those two specific words.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The alignment matrix can help us visualize when a model swaps the order of words during translation.
exercises.explain: An alignment matrix makes it easy to see when a model swapped word orders, because the bright spots in the grid will deviate from a straight diagonal line.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of the alignment matrix.
exercises.sentence: An alignment matrix is a visual grid created by plotting the attention weights between input and output words.
exercises.explain: The alignment matrix gets its data directly from attention weights, which measure the strength of the connection between words.

exercises.prompt: Which system describes an alignment matrix?

options.text: System A: \"A tool that forces the translation model to translate words in a strict one-to-one order.\"
System B: \"A visual grid that plots attention weights to show which words were connected during translation.\"
System C: \"A database that stores all translated sentences in alphabetical order.\"

exercises.explain: System A is incorrect because translation models do not just translate word-for-word in order; they need to align words even when word orders swap. System B correctly identifies the visual grid tool.

exercises.prompt: If you look at the structure of an alignment matrix grid, what do its two axes (rows and columns) represent?

options.text: It lists the vocabulary size of both languages.
It maps the input language words on one axis and the output language words on the other.
It tracks the speed of the training process over time.
It counts how many times the model made a translation error.

exercises.explain: The alignment matrix maps input positions to output positions. In a French-to-English translation, the matrix will have one axis representing the French words and the other axis representing the English words.
